habitat: Mountainous/hilly regions with rocks; adjacent woodlands; grasslands; shrublands; and savanna
story: An icon of the skies, the Cape Eagle Owl (Bubo capensis) is a species from the Strigidae family. It thrives in Mountainous/hilly regions with rocks; adjacent woodlands; grasslands; shrublands; and savanna, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Cape Eagle Owl is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Cape Eagle Owl helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Cape Eagle Owl, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
